name: Cursor
description: AI-native fork of VS Code; leading IDE agent

developer.comment: Built by Anysphere Inc., founded 2022 by four MIT students.

first-release.comment: Cursor was first launched publicly in March 2023 as a VS Code fork.

status.comment: Actively developed; reached 1.0 in June 2025 and shipping ongoing releases (2.4+ as of check; verified 2026-06-02).

license.comment: Closed-source product with a free Hobby tier plus paid plans. Forks open-source VS Code but the agent/editor itself is proprietary.

interface.comment: Primarily a standalone IDE; also ships a CLI and web/mobile interface for background agents.

host-ides.comment: Standalone forked editor (based on VS Code), not an extension running inside another IDE.

open-source.comment: The agent/editor itself is closed-source despite being built on open-source VS Code.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Standalone editor at its core; also offers a CLI and cloud-hosted Background Agents.

default-model-provider.comment: Auto mode picks across frontier models from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI; also ships its own Composer models.

model-choice.comment: Users can select among models from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI, and Cursor's own.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Supports BYOK; users can supply their own OpenAI/Anthropic API keys, and Teams plan references BYOK usage.

local-model-support.comment: Possible by overriding the OpenAI API base URL to a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint (e.g. Ollama); workable but unofficial/experimental and requires the model be reachable via a public-style endpoint.

multi-file-editing.comment: Agent plans and applies coordinated edits across multiple files.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Agent Mode loops: reads codebase, edits, runs commands, watches output, and iterates until the task is done.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Agent can execute terminal commands and monitor output.

mcp-support.comment: Shipped Model Context Protocol support including one-click install and OAuth (Cursor 1.0).

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Subagents feature shipped (changelog 2.4); Background Agents also allow multiple parallel cloud tasks.

codebase-indexing.comment: Builds a semantic index: chunks code, generates vector embeddings, and stores them for retrieval.

planning-mode.comment: Plan mode researches the codebase and produces an editable plan/to-do document to review and approve before execution.

multimodal-input.comment: Agent reads image files (.png, .jpg, .gif, .webp, .svg) as input.

browser-computer-use.comment: Agent has browser tools with screenshot and interaction capabilities to act and verify.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: Model-driven delegation: the parent agent 'proactively delegates tasks based on task complexity and scope' and uses the built-in Explore/Bash/Browser subagents 'automatically when appropriate' without the user invoking them; custom subagents with 'use proactively' descriptions are auto-selected too. Users can also invoke explicitly (e.g. /verifier). Verified 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Spans tab autocomplete, chat edits, supervised/autonomous Agent runs, and cloud Background Agents.

inline-completion.comment: Provides Tab autocomplete completions as you type.

background-async-execution.comment: Background Agents run tasks off-machine in the cloud while the developer does other work.

persistent-memory.comment: Memories (shipped 1.0) remembers facts from conversations and references them in the future.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Agent auto-creates checkpoints before significant changes; user can restore files to any checkpoint.

git-integration.comment: Native git awareness; Background Agents open branches and commit, BugBot reviews PRs.

pr-creation.comment: Background Agents read GitHub issues, open branches, commit, and draft pull requests.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports project-level rules (.cursor/rules/*.mdc, legacy .cursorrules, AGENTS.md) plus global User Rules.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Hooks run before/after agent actions and can block, modify, or audit behavior; also custom commands and a marketplace for rules/skills/plugins.

permission-model.comment: Defaults to asking approval for actions; auto-run / allowlist behavior is configurable per the rules and agent settings.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Cloud SaaS product; no documented self-host/on-prem deployment of the agent. Privacy mode keeps code local but the service is vendor-hosted.

privacy-mode.comment: Privacy Mode guarantees code data is never stored by model providers or used for training (verified 2026-06-02).

enterprise-controls.comment: Teams adds SAML/OIDC SSO; Enterprise adds audit logs, service accounts, and SCIM seat management (RBAC-style). No documented self-hosted VPC.

pricing-model.comment: Free Hobby tier, monthly subscriptions, plus usage-based credits for premium model usage (verified 2026-06-02).

free-tier.comment: Hobby plan is free with limited Agent requests and Tab completions, no credit card required.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Individual (Pro) is $20/mo. Cheapest paid plan; Teams is $40/user/mo (verified 2026-06-02).

maturity.comment: GA since 1.0 (June 2025), very widely deployed and polished; one of the leading IDE agents.

community-size.comment: Among the largest AI coding tools by adoption; Copilot/Cursor scale per the comparison thresholds.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: MCP server ecosystem plus a Cursor Marketplace bundling rules, skills, subagents, hooks, and plugins.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads project rules from .cursor/rules/*.mdc (and the legacy .cursorrules file) plus AGENTS.md in the project root and nested subdirectories. Does not use CLAUDE.md or copilot-instructions.

memory-scopes.comment: Team Rules (org/enterprise, Team/Enterprise plans), global User Rules, project rules (.cursor/rules), and folder-scoped AGENTS.md / nested rules. Precedence: Team > Project > User.

scoped-rules-files.comment: Rules activate by glob match (auto-attached when a matching file is in context), by agent relevance detection (description, no globs), or always (alwaysApply: true). Also manual @rule-name mention. No file-type-specific mode beyond globs.

auto-memory.comment: Memories: a background model proposes cross-session facts (approved before saving), stored per project. Toggle at Cursor Settings → Rules → 'Generate Memories'.

skill-support.comment: Agent Skills (SKILL.md) — reusable, on-demand packaged instructions/scripts the agent loads when relevant; plus custom /-triggered commands. Shipped in 2.4 with /create-skill and /migrate-to-skills.

skill-invocation.comment: Model autonomously selects relevant skills by default; users can also invoke via the / slash menu. Setting disable-model-invocation: true restricts a skill to explicit slash-command use only.

always-on-surface.comment: Progressive disclosure: skill metadata (name/description) is presented to the agent for relevance decisions, and the full SKILL.md body plus resources load on demand only when needed.

skill-distribution.comment: Manual files in .cursor/skills/ or .agents/skills/ (recursive discovery), import from GitHub repos via Settings, and distribution through the Cursor Marketplace (plugins bundle skills). Built on the open agentskills.io standard.

sandboxing-technology.comment: Local agent runs in a kernel-level OS sandbox: macOS Seatbelt via sandbox-exec, Linux Landlock (filesystem) + seccomp (syscall blocking), Windows via WSL2; writes confined to the workspace overlay. Cloud Background Agents run in Docker containers on AWS VMs. Verified 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: permissions.json gates MCP tools (server:tool) and terminal commands by prefix match (e.g. `git`, `npm:install*`). The CLI permissions engine adds Read(path/glob), Write(path/glob) separate read-vs-write gating, and WebFetch(domain) per-network-domain rules. Verified 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: The CLI permissions engine has explicit allow and deny lists where 'Deny rules take precedence over allow rules'. The in-app permissions.json is allowlist-only; deny-overrides is the most expressive declarative model. No specificity-based re-override or user-ordered evaluation documented. Verified 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: Beyond run-mode presets and declarative allow/deny rules, the beforeShellExecution / beforeMCPExecution / beforeReadFile hooks act as a programmable decision point: they receive the concrete action and return allow/deny/ask (or modify input), so policy can be scripted per action. Verified 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No shipped default deny pattern for .env/key files in the IDE: file access is governed by user-authored .cursorignore blocklists, and the CLI's Read(.env*) / Write(**/*.key) protections appear only as example deny rules the user must add. The sandbox does protect .git/config, .git/hooks and .cursorignore from writes, but generic secret files are not gated by default. Verified 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: terminalAllowlist / mcpAllowlist in ~/.cursor/permissions.json (per-user) or <workspace>/.cursor/permissions.json (per-repo) persist across sessions; files are read on startup and re-read on change. In-app allowlist entries are likewise saved. Verified 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: 'Run Everything' run mode executes all terminal commands with no approval prompts and no safety classifier in the loop — a documented full-autonomy/YOLO mode. Verified 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: The sandbox restricts internet access by default and prompts when the agent needs to step outside it. Network access is configurable to three levels via sandbox.json: domains in the allowlist only, allowlist plus Cursor's built-in defaults, or allow all; Enterprise admins can enforce org-wide allow/deny egress policy. Verified 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box (Auto-review, default in 3.6+): file reads and code searches need no approval and the agent applies edits, but terminal commands require explicit approval unless allowlisted or classified safe; config-file modifications also prompt. Verified 2026-06-12.
